page_content:
{"Question": "How do I initialize an operations pipeline with my experiment data?", "Context": "I have a DataFrame with A/B test results and want to start analyzing it with OperationsPipe.", "Code_Example": "import pandas as pd
from operations_tools import OperationsPipe

# Load your experiment data
experiment_df = pd.read_csv('experiment_results.csv')

# Initialize the pipeline with your data
pipe = OperationsPipe.from_dataframe(experiment_df)

# Now you can chain operations on this pipeline
# For example:
# pipe | PercentChange('variant', 'control')", "Usage": "OperationsPipe.from_dataframe(df)"}

metadata.description: Initialize an operations pipeline with a DataFrame

page_content:
{"Question": "How do I calculate the percent change between treatment and control groups?", "Context": "I want to see how much my treatment group's conversion rate differs from the control group in percentage terms.", "Code_Example": "from operations_tools import OperationsPipe, PercentChange, ShowOperation

# Initialize pipeline with experiment data
pipe = OperationsPipe.from_dataframe(experiment_df)

# Calculate percent change for all numeric metrics
result_pipe = pipe | PercentChange('variant', 'control')

# View the results
percent_changes = result_pipe | ShowOperation()
print(percent_changes)  # Shows percent changes for all metrics", "Usage": "PercentChange('variant', 'control')"}

metadata.description: Calculate percent change between treatment and control

page_content:
{"Question": "How do I calculate the absolute difference between test variants and control?", "Context": "I need to know the raw difference in revenue between my test groups and control group.", "Code_Example": "from operations_tools import OperationsPipe, AbsoluteChange, ShowOperation

# Initialize pipeline with experiment data
pipe = OperationsPipe.from_dataframe(experiment_df)

# Calculate absolute change for all numeric metrics
result_pipe = pipe | AbsoluteChange('variant', 'control')

# View the results
absolute_changes = result_pipe | ShowOperation()
print(absolute_changes)  # Shows absolute changes for all metrics", "Usage": "AbsoluteChange('variant', 'control')"}

metadata.description: Calculate absolute change between treatment and control

page_content:
{"Question": "How do I perform a stratified analysis using the Mantel-Haenszel estimator?", "Context": "I need to control for user segments in my A/B test analysis to account for confounding variables.", "Code_Example": "from operations_tools import OperationsPipe, MH, ShowOperation

# Initialize pipeline with experiment data
pipe = OperationsPipe.from_dataframe(experiment_df)

# Apply Mantel-Haenszel estimator stratified by user segment
result_pipe = pipe | MH('variant', 'control', 'user_segment')

# View the results
mh_results = result_pipe | ShowOperation()
print(mh_results)  # Shows MH estimates for all metrics stratified by user segment", "Usage": "MH('variant', 'control', 'user_segment')"}

metadata.description: Calculate Mantel-Haenszel estimator for stratified analysis

page_content:
{"Question": "How do I use CUPED variance reduction in my experiment analysis?", "Context": "I want to improve statistical power by using pre-experiment data as a covariate in my analysis.", "Code_Example": "from operations_tools import OperationsPipe, CUPED, ShowOperation

# Initialize pipeline with experiment data (containing both pre and post metrics)
pipe = OperationsPipe.from_dataframe(experiment_df)

# Apply CUPED using pre-experiment revenue as covariate
result_pipe = pipe | CUPED('variant', 'control', 'pre_revenue')

# View the results
cuped_results = result_pipe | ShowOperation()
print(cuped_results)  # Shows CUPED-adjusted metrics", "Usage": "CUPED('variant', 'control', 'pre_revenue')"}

metadata.description: Apply CUPED variance reduction to experiment analysis

page_content:
{"Question": "How do I implement a pre-post analysis for my experiment?", "Context": "I want to compare metrics before and after the experiment to measure treatment effect.", "Code_Example": "from operations_tools import OperationsPipe, PrePostChange, ShowOperation

# Initialize pipeline with experiment data (containing both pre and post metrics)
pipe = OperationsPipe.from_dataframe(experiment_df)

# Apply PrePost analysis comparing pre-usage and post-usage metrics
result_pipe = pipe | PrePostChange('variant', 'control', 'pre_usage')

# View the results
prepost_results = result_pipe | ShowOperation()
print(prepost_results)  # Shows pre-post analysis results", "Usage": "PrePostChange('variant', 'control', 'pre_usage')"}

metadata.description: Use PrePost approach for experiment analysis

page_content:
{"Question": "How do I filter data to focus on specific test variants?", "Context": "I want to analyze only certain variants from my multi-variant test.", "Code_Example": "from operations_tools import OperationsPipe, FilterConditions

# Initialize pipeline with experiment data
pipe = OperationsPipe.from_dataframe(experiment_df)

# Filter to include only control and treatment_A variants
filtered_pipe = pipe | FilterConditions(['control', 'treatment_A'])

# Continue analysis with the filtered data
# For example: filtered_pipe | PercentChange('variant', 'control')", "Usage": "FilterConditions(['control', 'treatment_A'])"}

metadata.description: Filter data to focus on specific test variants

page_content:
{"Question": "How do I perform power analysis for my experiment?", "Context": "I need to determine if my sample size is sufficient to detect a meaningful effect.", "Code_Example": "from operations_tools import OperationsPipe, PowerAnalysis, ShowOperation

# Initialize pipeline with experiment data
pipe = OperationsPipe.from_dataframe(experiment_df)

# Perform power analysis for conversion_rate metric
result_pipe = pipe | PowerAnalysis('variant', 'control', 'conversion_rate', alpha=0.05, power=0.8)

# View the results
power_results = result_pipe | ShowOperation()
print(power_results)  # Shows required sample sizes and actual power", "Usage": "PowerAnalysis('variant', 'control', 'conversion_rate', alpha=0.05, power=0.8)"}

metadata.description: Perform power analysis for experiment sample size requirements

page_content:
{"Question": "How do I create a stratified summary of my experiment metrics?", "Context": "I want to analyze how my experiment performed across different user segments.", "Code_Example": "from operations_tools import OperationsPipe, StratifiedSummary, ShowOperation

# Initialize pipeline with experiment data
pipe = OperationsPipe.from_dataframe(experiment_df)

# Create stratified summary by user segment
result_pipe = pipe | StratifiedSummary('variant', 'user_segment', metrics=['conversion_rate', 'revenue'])

# View the results
stratified_summary = result_pipe | ShowOperation()
print(stratified_summary)  # Shows metrics summarized by user segment and variant", "Usage": "StratifiedSummary('variant', 'user_segment', metrics=['conversion_rate', 'revenue'])"}

metadata.description: Create a stratified summary of experiment metrics

page_content:
{"Question": "How do I calculate bootstrap confidence intervals for my experiment results?", "Context": "I want to get robust confidence intervals without assuming normal distribution.", "Code_Example": "from operations_tools import OperationsPipe, BootstrapCI, ShowOperation

# Initialize pipeline with experiment data
pipe = OperationsPipe.from_dataframe(experiment_df)

# Calculate bootstrap confidence intervals for conversion_rate
result_pipe = pipe | BootstrapCI('variant', 'control', 'conversion_rate', confidence=0.95, n_bootstraps=1000)

# View the results
bootstrap_results = result_pipe | ShowOperation()
print(bootstrap_results)  # Shows bootstrap CIs and p-values", "Usage": "BootstrapCI('variant', 'control', 'conversion_rate', confidence=0.95, n_bootstraps=1000)"}

metadata.description: Calculate bootstrap confidence intervals for experiment metrics

page_content:
{"Question": "How do I adjust p-values for multiple comparisons?", "Context": "I'm testing multiple metrics and need to control the false discovery rate.", "Code_Example": "from operations_tools import OperationsPipe, MultiComparisonAdjustment, ShowOperation

# Initialize pipeline with experiment data that includes p-values
# (e.g., after running BootstrapCI which adds p-values)
pipe = OperationsPipe.from_dataframe(results_with_pvalues_df)

# Adjust p-values using Benjamini-Hochberg method
result_pipe = pipe | MultiComparisonAdjustment('p_value', method='fdr')

# View the results
adjusted_results = result_pipe | ShowOperation()
print(adjusted_results)  # Shows original and adjusted p-values", "Usage": "MultiComparisonAdjustment('p_value', method='fdr')"}

{"Question": "How do I execute all operations in my pipeline?", "Context": "I've set up multiple operations and want to get all the results at once.", "Code_Example": "from operations_tools import OperationsPipe, PercentChange, AbsoluteChange, ExecuteOperations

# Initialize pipeline with experiment data
pipe = OperationsPipe.from_dataframe(experiment_df)

# Add multiple operations to the pipeline
pipe = (pipe
       | PercentChange('variant', 'control', output_name='pct_change')
       | AbsoluteChange('variant', 'control', output_name='abs_change'))

# Execute all operations and get results
all_results = pipe | ExecuteOperations()

# Access individual results
print(all_results['pct_change'])
print(all_results['abs_change'])", "Usage": "pipe | ExecuteOperations()"}

metadata.description: Execute the operations pipeline and get all results

page_content:
{"Question": "How can I create complex analysis pipelines for my experiment?", "Context": "I need to chain multiple operations to perform sophisticated analysis on my A/B test data.", "Code_Example": "from operations_tools import OperationsPipe, FilterConditions, PercentChange, BootstrapCI, ExecuteOperations

# Initialize pipeline with experiment data
pipe = OperationsPipe.from_dataframe(experiment_df)

# Create a complex analysis pipeline
results_pipe = (pipe
    # Filter to specific platforms
    | FilterConditions(['control', 'treatment_A', 'treatment_B'])
    
    # Calculate percent changes for all metrics
    | PercentChange('variant', 'control', output_name='pct_changes')
    
    # Calculate bootstrap confidence intervals for conversion rate
    | BootstrapCI('variant', 'control', 'conversion_rate', 
                confidence=0.95, n_bootstraps=1000,
                output_name='conversion_bootstrap')
)

# Execute all operations and get results
all_results = results_pipe | ExecuteOperations()

# Access individual analyses
print('Percent Changes:')
print(all_results['pct_changes'])

print('
Bootstrap Results for Conversion:')
print(all_results['conversion_bootstrap'])", "Usage": "Chain multiple operations with the | operator to create complex analysis pipelines"}

metadata.description: Chain multiple operations to create complex analysis pipelines
